skill.name: voice-system-expert
skill.description: Guides safe changes to the Quetrex voice interface, including WebRTC audio flow, OpenAI Realtime API usage, and browser echo cancellation.
skill.summary: Helps developers preserve Quetrex voice architecture while improving WebRTC voice features.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, documentation references, and prose substrings as shell execution, Windows SAM access, and weak cryptography. Review found no executable scripts, network calls, filesystem writes, secret access, prompt injection text, or malicious intent; the skill only allows Read and provides architecture guidance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer matched inline backticks and fenced Markdown blocks that document TypeScript examples and file names. SKILL.md grants only Read access and does not instruct the agent to execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are Markdown formatting, TypeScript snippets, or documentation paths. No command execution API or shell instruction is present in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Windows SAM Pattern in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The flagged lines refer to Quetrex documentation paths and voice system prose, not the Windows SAM database. No credential database path, registry access, or password extraction behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context is project documentation and architecture guidance. There is no reference to Windows account databases or any instruction to read sensitive operating system files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography matches occur in metadata and explanatory text. The skill contains no cryptographic implementation, hashing routine, encryption call, or recommendation to use weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are natural language or front matter, not executable cryptographic code. No MD5, DES, SHA-1, or similar weak algorithm usage is implemented or recommended.

content.value_statement: Voice changes can break echo cancellation, WebRTC audio flow, or OpenAI Realtime behavior. This skill gives developers Quetrex-specific rules for safe voice interface changes.
actual_capabilities: Explains Quetrex voice architecture and required documentation to review before changes.
Identifies the always-on microphone pattern used with browser echo cancellation.
Lists voice implementation areas that can be changed safely.
Warns against microphone toggling, custom echo cancellation, and native audio bypasses.
Provides testing guidance for browsers, permissions, network issues, and echo scenarios.
Points developers to key Quetrex files and functions for voice work.
limitations: It is specific to Quetrex architecture and may not apply to unrelated voice systems.
It provides guidance only and does not inspect source code automatically.
It does not replace real browser and device testing for voice changes.
It assumes the project remains a web application using browser WebRTC behavior.

use_cases.title: Review a Voice Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Check whether a proposed fix keeps the microphone always enabled and preserves browser echo cancellation.

use_cases.title: Plan OpenAI Realtime Changes
use_cases.description: Identify safe areas to modify before changing WebRTC setup, connection handling, or audio response playback.

use_cases.title: Validate Release Testing
use_cases.description: Build a focused browser test checklist for echo, permissions, disconnects, and platform coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Simple Voice Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before editing voice code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this planned Quetrex voice change. Tell me whether it keeps the always-on microphone pattern and browser echo cancellation intact.

prompt_templates.title: Find Risky Audio Patterns
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a pull request
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this voice interface change for patterns that could break WebRTC audio, echo cancellation, or OpenAI Realtime turn detection.

prompt_templates.title: Design Safe Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design Quetrex voice error handling for denied microphone permission, missing devices, and reconnect failures without changing audio routing.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Voice Architecture Proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this proposed Quetrex voice architecture change against ADR-001 and the documented browser AEC approach. List approval criteria and blockers.

output_examples.input: Can I pause the microphone while the assistant speaks?
output_examples.output: Recommendation: do not pause the microphone track. Keep the microphone enabled and rely on browser echo cancellation with server-side voice activity detection.

output_examples.input: What should I test after changing connection retry logic?
output: Confirm speaker echo does not trigger feedback.

output_examples.input: Is an AudioWorklet bypass a good fix for playback issues?
output_examples.output: Recommendation: avoid routing audio outside the browser pipeline. That approach can break browser echo cancellation and reintroduce feedback loops.

best_practices: Read the voice ADR and architecture documents before changing voice behavior.
Keep microphone input and speaker output inside the browser audio pipeline.
Test voice changes on real browsers with high speaker volume and permission edge cases.
anti_patterns: Do not toggle the microphone track to avoid echo.
Do not bypass browser audio with native routing or AudioWorklet workarounds.
Do not add artificial delays as a substitute for correct echo cancellation.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill declares Read-only tool access and contains documentation guidance.

faq.question: What voice architecture does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends an always-on microphone with browser echo cancellation and WebRTC audio flow.

faq.question: Can I change voice UI feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill identifies UI indicators and user messages as safe areas to improve.

faq.question: Can I implement custom echo cancellation?
faq.answer: No. The skill warns against custom echo cancellation because browser AEC is the selected architecture.

faq.question: Does it support non-Quetrex projects?
faq.answer: It may be useful as reference, but its guidance is written for Quetrex-specific decisions.

faq.question: What should be tested before release?
faq.answer: Test real browsers, echo behavior, microphone permissions, poor networks, and mid-conversation disconnects.
